title: dioscorea biloba subsp. coquimbana
intro: Dioscorea biloba subsp. coquimbana, also known as Chilean wild yam, is a sub-species of the Dioscorea biloba plant, belonging to the family Dioscoreaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dioscorea biloba subsp. coquimbana include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause, improve skin health, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including capsules, teas, and tinctures, making it a versatile addition to traditional medicine and wellness practices.
